_comment: Pure-CPU reference numbers reported by GitHub user pestopoppa in PR 1744 comments. Reproduced verbatim for comparison; we do NOT own this data.
notes: Pure-CPU CPU+memory bandwidth pressure: Q4_K_M target consumes ~18 GiB, drafter ~510 MiB, KV cache ~3.5 GiB.
EPYC 9655's 12-channel DDR5-6400 (614 GB/s aggregate) is what makes the 7 t/s baseline possible at all.

Speedup is HIGHER on CPU than GPU because the drafter's relative cost is dominated by FFN, which is memory-bound — same HBM/DDR walls, so the drafter's tiny weight set fits in cache and runs essentially free.
This is the workload pestopoppa was trying to fix when they identified the segfault in params_use_gemma4_external_mtp.
